title: Adaptive dashboard filtering and empty states
prompt: Improve the AWS learning platform adaptive dashboard with saved filters, clear empty states, and keyboard-accessible filtering. Keep the existing tutor and quiz flows working.
judgeFocus: preservation of tutor and quiz behavior
whether repair loops used concrete runtime evidence

scale.1: Fails the dimension or has no usable evidence.
scale.2: Has major gaps, regressions, or mostly claim-based evidence.
scale.3: Acceptable but incomplete, noisy, or only partially evidenced.
scale.4: Strong result with minor gaps or limited missing evidence.
scale.5: Excellent result with complete behavior and strong evidence.

criticalRequirements: Dashboard filtering must be saved, visible, keyboard-accessible, and scoped to the adaptive dashboard.
Empty states must be clear and user-facing for dashboard sections affected by filtering or missing data.
Tutor and quiz flows must remain behaviorally intact.
Harness planning must fit the configured sprint budget without relying on post-planning truncation.

dimensions.description: How completely the final workspace satisfies the dashboard filtering, saved state, empty state, accessibility, and tutor/quiz preservation request.
Whether typecheck, tests, smoke evidence, state persistence, keyboard behavior, and existing tutor/quiz behavior hold without introduced regressions.
Maintainability, scope control, idiomatic React/state handling, localStorage resilience, styling fit, and focused test coverage.
Quality of research, sprint-budget-aware planning, contract specificity, handoffs, repair behavior, and provider coordination.
Whether harness verdicts and final eval claims are grounded in concrete artifacts, commands, browser/runtime evidence when available, and clear treatment of baseline failures.

scoringNotes: Do not change this rubric based on run-generated eval criteria or contracts.
Pre-existing fixture failures should be recorded but should not dominate correctness unless the run worsens or hides them.
Missing browser evidence should reduce evaluationTrustworthiness when the task has visible UI behavior.
